en: It was not until the discovery of the Rosetta Stone that some clues to the Egyptian language became known.
ja: ロゼッタストーンが発見されて初めて、エジプトの言語を解く手がかりがいくらか知られるようになった。

questions.q: "It was not until the discovery of the Rosetta Stone that some clues to the Egyptian language became known." の "It was ... that ..." と同じ使い方をしている文はどれか。
questions.a: It was surprising that he finished all the work in one day.
questions.b: It was so hot that I opened all the windows in the room.
questions.c: It was last Sunday that we cleaned the beach together.
questions.d: It was said that the old house was built 100 years ago.
questions.explanation: この文は It is ... that ... の強調構文で、not until the discovery of the Rosetta Stone の部分を that の前に置いて強調している。cは last Sunday を強調する同じ強調構文。aとdは that 以下が本当の主語になる形式主語構文、bは so ... that ...(とても~なので…)で、どれも強調構文ではない。

questions.tag: パラフレーズ(not until の論理)
questions.q: "It was not until the discovery of the Rosetta Stone that some clues to the Egyptian language became known." の内容を最も正しく表している英文はどれか。
questions.a: Before the Rosetta Stone was found, people did not have any clues to the Egyptian language.
questions.b: People had some clues to the Egyptian language until the Rosetta Stone was found.
questions.c: The Rosetta Stone was not found until people got some clues to the Egyptian language.
questions.d: After the Rosetta Stone was found, clues to the Egyptian language still remained unknown.
questions.explanation: not until X that Y は「Xまでは Y ではなかった=Xして初めてYになった」なので、発見前は手がかりが全く無かったというaが正解。bは「発見までは手がかりがあった」と until の論理を逆に処理した誤り。cは「手がかりが得られるまで発見されなかった」と原因と結果を入れ替えた誤り、dは否定のかかる範囲を発見後にずらした誤りである。

questions.q: "some clues to the Egyptian language" の "to" と同じ使い方をしている文はどれか。
questions.a: She was glad to get a letter from her old friend.
questions.b: He finally found the key to the mystery.
questions.c: They walked to the museum in the heavy rain.
questions.d: I still have a lot of things to tell you.
questions.explanation: clues to ~ の to は「~に対する・~を解くための」というつながりを表す前置詞で、the key to the mystery(謎を解くカギ)の to と同じ用法。cの to は「~へ」という方向を表す前置詞で意味が異なる。aは感情の原因を表す不定詞、dは名詞を修飾する不定詞の to であり、どちらも前置詞ではない。
